heading: Powers of Commission
label: 30107 Powers of Commission
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsecs. (a)(6), (8), and (e), means the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971, as defined by .section 30101 of this title

CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Congress, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section. Some section numbers referenced in amendment notes below reflect the classification of such sections prior to their editorial reclassification to this title.section 437d of Title 2

children.text: to require by special or general orders, any person to submit, under oath, such written reports and answers to questions as the Commission may prescribe;
to require by subpena, signed by the chairman or the vice chairman, the attendance and testimony of witnesses and the production of all documentary evidence relating to the execution of its duties;

in any proceeding or investigation, to order testimony to be taken by deposition before any person who is designated by the Commission and has the power to administer oaths and, in such instances, to compel testimony and the production of evidence in the same manner as authorized under paragraph (3);
to pay witnesses the same fees and mileage as are paid in like circumstances in the courts of the United States;

to initiate (through civil actions for injunctive, declaratory, or other appropriate relief), defend (in the case of any civil action brought under ) or appeal any civil action in the name of the Commission to enforce the provisions of this Act and chapter 95 and chapter 96 of title 26, through its general counsel;section 30109(a)(8) of this title
to render advisory opinions under ;section 30108 of this title

to develop such prescribed forms and to make, amend, and repeal such rules, pursuant to the provisions of chapter 5 of title 5, as are necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act and chapter 95 and chapter 96 of title 26; and
to conduct investigations and hearings expeditiously, to encourage voluntary compliance, and to report apparent violations to the appropriate law enforcement authorities.

children.heading: Judicial orders for compliance with subpenas and orders of Commission; contempt of court
children.label: b Judicial orders for compliance with subpenas and orders of Commission; contempt of court
children.text: Upon petition by the Commission, any United States district court within the jurisdiction of which any inquiry is being carried on may, in case of refusal to obey a subpena or order of the Commission issued under subsection (a), issue an order requiring compliance. Any failure to obey the order of the court may be punished by the court as a contempt thereof.

children.heading: Civil liability for disclosure of information
children.label: c Civil liability for disclosure of information
children.text: No person shall be subject to civil liability to any person (other than the Commission or the United States) for disclosing information at the request of the Commission.

children.heading: Concurrent transmissions to Congress or Member of budget estimates, etc.; prior submission of legislative recommendations, testimony, or comments on legislation
children.label: d Concurrent transmissions to Congress or Member of budget estimates, etc.; prior submission of legislative recommendations, testimony, or comments on legislation

children.text: Whenever the Commission submits any budget estimate or request to the President or the Office of Management and Budget, it shall concurrently transmit a copy of such estimate or request to the Congress.

Whenever the Commission submits any legislative recommendation, or testimony, or comments on legislation, requested by the Congress or by any Member of the Congress, to the President or the Office of Management and Budget, it shall concurrently transmit a copy thereof to the Congress or to the Member requesting the same. No officer or agency of the United States shall have any authority to require the Commission to submit its legislative recommendations, testimony, or comments on legislation, to any office or agency of the United States for approval, comments, or review, prior to the submission of such recommendations, testimony, or comments to the Congress.

children.heading: Exclusive civil remedy for enforcement
children.label: e Exclusive civil remedy for enforcement
children.text: Except as provided in , the power of the Commission to initiate civil actions under subsection (a)(6) shall be the exclusive civil remedy for the enforcement of the provisions of this Act.section 30109(a)(8) of this title
